edges.description: Reduced 5-oxoprolinase activity blocks clearance of 5-oxo-L-proline.
OPLAH encodes the enzyme measured as reduced 5-oxoprolinase activity.
OPLAH molecular dysfunction is directly reflected by reduced measured 5-oxoprolinase activity.
Failure to clear 5-oxo-L-proline causes elevated urinary 5-oxoproline.
Urinary L-pyroglutamic acid is the HPO phenotype corresponding to accumulated 5-oxoproline.
Metabolic acidosis is reported in OPLAH deficiency, but the causal connection to 5-oxoprolinuria is incompletely resolved.
Hypoglycemia is an occasional Orphanet phenotype with uncertain mechanistic connection to OPLAH deficiency.
Feeding problems occur in symptomatic 5-oxoprolinuria reports, but penetrance and causality remain uncertain.
Failure to thrive is an occasional reported manifestation in the variable symptomatic spectrum.
Developmental delay is reported in symptomatic patients, but larger series note incomplete segregation with 5-oxoprolinuria.
Hypotonia is an occasional reported neurologic phenotype with uncertain direct mechanism.
Seizures are reported in symptomatic patients, but a direct biochemical mechanism is not established.
Speech delay is part of the reported variable neurologic spectrum.
Brain atrophy has been reported in a symptomatic OPLAH-deficient patient, but causality remains case-level.
Postnatal macrocephaly has been reported in a symptomatic patient and in Orphanet, without a defined intermediate mechanism.
Hypomyelination was reported in a symptomatic patient with elevated urinary 5-oxoproline.
Ventriculomegaly was reported in a symptomatic OPLAH-deficient patient, but mechanism is unresolved.
Corpus callosum hypoplasia was reported in a symptomatic OPLAH-deficient patient, with uncertain causality.

edges.description: Elevated urinary 5-oxoproline directly reports accumulation of the substrate that is not cleared by deficient OPLAH activity.
Urinary 5-oxoproline is the measured metabolite represented clinically as increased urinary L-pyroglutamic acid.
Reduced 5-oxoprolinase activity is the direct functional readout of deficient OPLAH molecular function.
Reduced enzyme activity is the biochemical measurement represented by the reduced circulating 5-oxoprolinase activity phenotype.

nodes.description: Reported affected individuals carry homozygous or compound heterozygous OPLAH variants, many private to individual families. Published functional work supports activity loss for selected missense variants, but clinical penetrance remains uncertain.

nodes.description: The gamma-glutamyl cycle mediates glutathione synthesis and degradation. Impaired 5-oxoprolinase activity causes accumulation of 5-oxo-L-proline, also called pyroglutamic acid, producing the diagnostic 5-oxoprolinuria.

nodes.description: No disease-modifying therapy is established for OPLAH deficiency. Management is therefore centered on long-term observation and standard symptomatic care for manifestations such as epilepsy, developmental delay, and speech delay. A reported antioxidant and cofactor regimen did not improve the two OPLAH-deficient patients in one mixed 5-oxoprolinuria series, so this entry does not model that regimen as an effective OPLAH-directed therapy.
